login.title: BorgScale
login.subtitle: Enter your credentials to continue
login.successChangePassword: Login successful! Please change your password.
login.totpPrompt: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app. Recovery codes also work here.
login.totpHint: Use your current authenticator code or a recovery code.
login.passkeyCancelled: Passkey sign-in was cancelled or no matching passkey was available
login.tagline: Secure, deduplicated backups - managed from one elegant interface.

firstLoginSetup.title: Set a new password or continue
firstLoginSetup.description: You can set a new password now or skip this step and continue to the dashboard.
firstLoginSetup.flowHint: After this step, BorgScale will continue with passkeys, analytics consent, and any active announcements.
firstLoginSetup.skipHint: You can skip for now and change it later from account settings.

archives.mountTimeout: Mount timeout: Large repositories may need more time to mount.
archives.restoreStarted: Restore job started! Check the Activity tab to monitor progress.
archives.mountCommandDesc: Open this command in a terminal to browse the mount folder.

restore.noArchives: No archives found in this repository

dialog.title: Restore Archive
dialog.fullRestoreWarning: This will restore the entire archive to the destination path. Existing files may be overwritten.

schedule.title: Schedule
schedule.subtitle: Manage automated backups and repository checks
schedule.noRepositories: You need to create at least one repository before scheduling backups.

repositories.title: Repository Management
repositories.subtitle: A repository is where your backed-up data will be stored. The files from your configured sources will be backed up here.

empty.hint: Create a local repository or remote repository via SSH.

toasts.dryRunCompleted: Dry run completed - review results below

users.title: User Management
users.subtitle: Manage account records and repository-level access from one admin surface.

repositoryAccess.title: Repository Access
repositoryAccess.globalAccessDesc: Admin accounts inherit full access to all repositories. No per-repository grants needed.

accessSummary.adminRole: Admin role grants full access to all repositories.
accessSummary.defaultAccess: Default access is all repositories as {{role}}. Switch to selected repositories only if you want to limit them.
accessSummary.restricted: This user is currently restricted to selected repositories only.

roles.adminDescription: Admin: Full access to all features and settings
roles.operatorDescription: Operator: Can run backups, manage schedules. Cannot manage users or system settings

deleteDialog.title: Delete User
deleteDialog.message: Are you sure you want to delete the user {{username}}?

emptyState.title: No users found
emptyState.description: Create your first user to get started

users.planCaption: Community includes up to 5 users. Upgrade for more seats.

search.placeholder: Search by name, username or email…

filter.noMatchDescription: Try adjusting your search or filter criteria.

scope.title: Repository scope
scope.restrictedHint: Use this when you want to limit access to a few repositories only.
scope.futureInheritHint: Future repositories inherit this access automatically.
scope.restrictedAccess: Restricted access. This user only sees repositories assigned below.

alert.allAccessPrefix: This user currently has access to all repositories as
alert.allAccessSuffix: . Switch to selected repositories only if you want to restrict them.
alert.automaticAccessPrefix: This account currently has automatic access to all repositories as

empty.automaticCoverage: No repository-specific assignments. Automatic access covers current and future repositories.

grantAccess.title: Grant access
grantAccess.noRepositories: No repositories configured. Add a repository first.
grantAccess.allAssigned: All repositories are already assigned to this user.

account.title: User Settings
account.description: Manage your personal profile, credentials, and access preferences.

subtitle.individual: Your personal account settings, security, and access are managed here.
subtitle.enterprise: Personal profile is separate from the system-wide deployment identity.

profile.title: Personal profile
profile.description: Your own identity. Changes here do not affect the system-wide deployment identity.

setup.title: Finish account setup
setup.description: Change your password first, then update your personal profile details below.

deployment.title: Deployment profile
deployment.description: System-wide setting. Controls how this deployment presents itself to all users.
deployment.individualDesc: Show admin full name as the account identity.
deployment.enterpriseDesc: Show an organization name as the deployment identity.

security.title: Security
security.description: Strengthen this local account with phishing-resistant sign-in and a recovery path that fits how you actually use this device.
security.passwordUpdateHint: Action required: update your password before continuing.
security.changeCredentialsHint: Click to change your login credentials
security.totpDescription: Protect your local account with time-based one-time passwords.
security.totpDisabledHint: Enable TOTP to require a second factor at login.
security.totpSetupIntro: Confirm your password to start configuring an authenticator app for this account.
security.totpSetupInstructions: Save your recovery codes, add the secret to your authenticator app, then enter the current 6-digit code to finish setup.
security.totpQrCodeHint: Scan this code with your authenticator app. If scanning is unavailable, use the manual setup secret below.
security.totpDisableIntro: Confirm your password and enter an authenticator or recovery code to disable TOTP.
security.totpDisableHint: You can use either a 6-digit authenticator code or a recovery code.
security.passkeysDescription: Register WebAuthn passkeys for passwordless, phishing-resistant sign-in.
security.passkeysManageHint: Review, remove, or add another passkey for your regular devices.
security.passkeyEmptyHint: Sign in faster on trusted devices without passwords.
security.passkeySetupIntro: Confirm your current password, then complete the browser passkey prompt.
security.passkeyPromptTitle: Sign in faster with a passkey
security.passkeyPromptDescription: Replace your password with a passkey — tap your fingerprint or face to sign in instantly.
security.passkeyBenefitFastDesc: No typing — authenticate instantly with your device.
security.passkeyBenefitSecureDesc: Passkeys can't be stolen or leaked like passwords.
security.passkeyBenefitNoPasswordDesc: Works with Face ID, Touch ID, or your device PIN.

access.title: Access
access.description: Tokens and repository access live under the same account umbrella.

globalAccess.title: Global access
globalAccess.description: Admin accounts inherit full access to all repositories and settings.

sshConnections.title: Remote Machines
sshConnections.subtitle: Manage remote machines with storage monitoring and logical mount points

singleKeySystem.title: Single-Key System
singleKeySystem.description: This system uses one SSH key for all remote connections. Generate the system key once, then deploy it to as many remote servers as needed.

systemKey.title: System SSH Key
systemKey.noKey: No system SSH key found. Generate a new one or import an existing one.

deployDialog.title: Deploy SSH Key to Server
deployDialog.sftpMode: Use SFTP mode for key deployment
deployDialog.useSudoHint: Prefix borg with sudo on the remote host. Requires passwordless sudo for the SSH user. Only applies to remote SSH backup mode.

instructions.title: Before adding this connection:
instructions.step1: Copy the public key from above

deleteKeyDialog.title: Delete System SSH Key
deleteKeyDialog.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this SSH key?
deleteKeyDialog.warning1: Remove the SSH key from database and filesystem

scripts.title: Script Library
scripts.subtitle: Reusable scripts for backup hooks and maintenance
scripts.empty: No scripts created yet. Scripts can be assigned to repositories for pre-backup and post-backup hooks.

runOn.warning: Warning - Only after backups with warnings
runOn.note: Note: The 'Run On' condition only applies to Post-Backup hooks.

fields.name: Name
fields.parametersHint: Check the box for parameters that contain sensitive data.
fields.nameHelperText: A unique name for this script
fields.descriptionHelperText: Optional description of what this script does

scripts.usedInPlaces: This script is used in {{count}} place(s). Changes will affect all assignments.

testDialog.title: Test Script
testDialog.hasParams: This script has parameters. Provide values below to test.
testDialog.noParams: This script has no parameters. Click 'Run Test' to execute it.

scripts.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"?

warnings.removeRecord: This will remove the job record from the system
warnings.noAffect: This will NOT affect any actual backup data
warnings.historyRemoved: Job history will be permanently removed
warnings.logsDeleted: Associated log files will be deleted

deleteArchive.title: Delete Archive?
deleteArchive.subtitle: Are you sure you want to delete this archive?
deleteArchive.warning: This action cannot be undone. The archive data will be permanently removed from the repository.

deleteSchedule.title: Delete Schedule?
deleteSchedule.message: Are you sure you want to delete this schedule?

cancelJob.title: Cancel Job?
cancelJob.message: Are you sure you want to cancel this job?

checkWarning.title: Check Repository Integrity
checkWarning.message: Running a full repository check can take a long time for large repositories.
checkWarning.otherOperationsUnavailable: Other operations (info, archives, backup) will not be available until check completes
checkWarning.progressTrackingDetail: You can monitor progress in real-time. Note: Check operations cannot be cancelled once started.
checkWarning.otherReposAccessible: Other repositories will remain accessible during this operation.
checkWarning.maxDurationHelper: Maximum time for the check operation. Default: 3600 seconds (1 hour). Set to 0 for unlimited.
checkWarning.maxDurationHelperBorg2: For Borg 2, setting a time limit runs a partial repository-only check. Use 0 for a full repository and archive check.
checkWarning.maxDurationHelperBorg2Short: Timed Borg 2 checks cover the repository phase only. Use 0 for a full check.
checkWarning.borg2PartialCheckNotice: Borg 2 only supports time-limited checks for the repository phase. Archive consistency checks run only when the duration is set to 0.

compactWarning.title: Compact Repository
compactWarning.message: Compacting the repository will free up space but may take some time.

lockError.title: Repository Locked
lockError.message: The repository is currently locked. This may be due to an interrupted operation.
lockError.breakLockWarning: Are you CERTAIN no backup or operation is currently running on this repository? Breaking the lock while an operation is running can corrupt your repository!
lockError.lockRemovedSuccess: Lock removed successfully! You can now retry your operation.
lockError.lockedDescription: {{repositoryName}} is locked by another process or has a stale lock.
lockError.staleLockInfo: If no backup is currently running, this is likely a stale lock from a crashed backup.
lockError.causeInterrupted: Previous backup was interrupted or crashed
lockError.causeNetworkDrop: Network connection dropped during SSH backup
lockError.causeContainerRestart: Container was restarted during an operation
lockError.causeCacheLocks: Repository cache locks from stale operations
lockError.beforeBreakingCheck1: Make sure no backup process is currently running
lockError.beforeBreakingCheck2: Check that no other client is accessing this repository
lockError.beforeBreakingCheck3: This will break both repository and cache locks
lockError.adminRequiredDetail: Only administrators can break repository locks. Please contact your admin for assistance.

repositoryInfo.title: Repository Information
repositoryInfo.noBackupsDesc: This repository has been initialized but contains no archives. Storage statistics will appear here after you create your first backup.
repositoryInfo.loadFailed: Failed to load repository information. Make sure the repository is accessible and properly initialized.
repositoryInfo.v2PlanRequired: This repository uses Borg 2. Your data is safe. Upgrade to Pro to view its details.

compact.explanation: Compaction removes unused segments and reclaims disk space from deleted archives.
compact.otherOperationsUnavailable: Other operations (info, archives, backup) will not be available until compaction completes
compact.progressTrackingDetail: You can monitor progress in real-time
compact.tip: Tip: Run compaction after pruning old archives to free up space. Other repositories will remain accessible.

mount.readOnlyInfo: The archive will be mounted as a read-only filesystem inside the container. You'll receive a command to access it via terminal.

prune.explanation: Pruning automatically deletes old archives based on retention rules. This helps manage repository size by keeping only the backups you need.
prune.dryRunTip: Tip: Always run "Dry Run" first to preview what will be deleted!
prune.retentionDescription: Specify how many backups to keep for each time period
prune.exampleExplanation: Example: With these settings, you'll keep the last 7 daily, 4 weekly, 6 monthly, and 1 yearly backup. Older archives will be deleted.
prune.dryRunSuccess: Dry run completed successfully. Review the output above to see which archives would be deleted. If everything looks correct, click "Prune Archives" to execute.
prune.warningTitle: Warning: Deleted archives cannot be recovered!
prune.warningCompact: After pruning, run "Compact" to actually free up disk space.
prune.dryRunNote: Archives listed above would be deleted. If this looks correct, run prune to apply.
prune.pruneNote: Pruning complete. Run Compact to reclaim disk space.
prune.noArchivesWouldBeDeleted: No archives would be deleted with these settings.

systemSettings.title: System Settings
systemSettings.subtitle: Configure system-wide settings, resource limits, and operation timeouts
systemSettings.operationTimeoutsDescription: Configure timeouts for various borg operations. Increase these for very large repositories (multi-terabyte, hundreds of archives) that may take longer to process.
systemSettings.repositoryMonitoringDescription: Configure automatic refresh of repository statistics. This keeps dashboard data up-to-date, especially useful for observe-only repositories where backups are managed externally.
systemSettings.maxConcurrentScheduledBackupsHelper: How many scheduled backup executions the shared scheduler may run at once. Set to 0 to pause scheduled backups.
systemSettings.maxConcurrentScheduledChecksHelper: How many scheduled repository checks the shared scheduler may run at once. Set to 0 to pause scheduled checks.
systemSettings.statsRefreshIntervalHelper: Repository stats (archive count, size, last backup) will be refreshed every {{interval}} minutes
systemSettings.manualRefreshAlert: Set to 0 to disable automatic refresh. The scheduler refreshes stats for all repositories, including observe-only ones where backups happen externally.
systemSettings.metricsAccessDescription: Control whether Prometheus metrics are exposed and whether scrapers must present a shared token.
systemSettings.metricsTokenDisabledHelper: Turn on token protection to require a shared secret for metrics scraping.
systemSettings.metricsTokenConfigured: A metrics token is already configured.
systemSettings.metricsTokenWillGenerate: A token will be generated the next time you save these settings.
systemSettings.metricsTokenWillRotate: A new metrics token will be generated when you save.
systemSettings.metricsHeaderHelp: Prometheus scrapers should send the token using X-Borg-Metrics-Token or Authorization: Bearer <token>.
systemSettings.metricsTokenDialogWarning: Copy this token now. You won't be able to see it again.
systemSettings.metricsTokenCloseBody: You haven't copied the token. Once you close this dialog, the token cannot be retrieved.
systemSettings.archiveBrowsingLimitsDescription: These limits prevent out-of-memory errors when browsing archives with millions of files. The borg process is terminated early if limits are exceeded.
systemSettings.proxyAuthDescription: Review the active proxy-auth configuration, trusted headers, and runtime safety warnings.
systemSettings.proxyAuthEnabledStatus: Proxy authentication is enabled. BorgScale expects your reverse proxy or identity provider to authenticate users and forward trusted headers.
systemSettings.proxyAuthDisabledStatus: Built-in BorgScale authentication is active. Proxy authentication is currently disabled.
systemSettings.maxFilesHelperText: Maximum files when browsing. Current: {{current}}M files
systemSettings.maxMemoryRangeError: Must be between {{min}} MB and {{max}} MB
systemSettings.maxMemoryHelperText: Maximum memory for browsing. Current: {{current}} GB
systemSettings.largeLimitsWarning: Increasing these limits significantly can cause out-of-memory errors and crash the server. Only increase if you have sufficient RAM and need to browse very large archives.

notifications.title: Notification Services
notifications.subtitle: Configure notification services for backup and restore alerts
notifications.noServicesSubtitle: Add your first notification service to get alerts about backup failures and other important events.
notifications.testSentSuccessfully: Test notification sent successfully! Check your service.
notifications.nameAndUrlRequired: Name and Service URL are required
notifications.alertDescription: Get notified about backup failures, restore completions, and scheduled job issues via 100+ services including Email, Slack, Discord, Telegram, Pushover, and more.

form.serviceUrlHelper: Use Apprise URL format. See examples above.
form.tipText: For automation and monitoring, use jsons:// or json:// URLs to automatically receive pure JSON data.
form.titlePrefixHelper: Add a custom prefix to all notification titles (e.g., '[Production] Backup Successful')
form.includeJobNameHelper: Adds job or schedule name to notification titles for easier identification (e.g., "Backup Successful - Daily Backup")
form.schedulerErrorsHelper: Notifies when the scheduler fails to start a backup (e.g., system errors). Regular backup failures are handled above.
form.selectRepositoriesHelper: Only send notifications for backups from these repositories

confirmDelete.messagePrefix: Are you sure you want to delete
confirmDelete.messageSuffix: ? You will no longer receive notifications from this service.

confirmDelete.messagePrefix: Are you sure you want to delete
confirmDelete.messageSuffix: ? You will no longer receive notifications from this service.

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.subtitle: Customize your experience and manage your privacy settings
preferences.analyticsDescription: Help us improve BorgScale by sharing anonymous usage data. We collect page views, feature clicks, and browser type, without tracking IP addresses, hostnames, cookies, or any personally identifiable information.
preferences.analyticsTransparencyAfterLink: to see exactly what we collect. Anonymous and privacy-focused.

betaFeatures.title: Beta Features
betaFeatures.subtitle: Enable experimental features (may be unstable)
betaFeatures.description: Try experimental features before they're released to everyone. These features are still in development and may change.
betaFeatures.enableBypassLocksInfo: Enable bypass-lock for all borg info commands
betaFeatures.bypassLocksInfoDescription: Adds --bypass-lock to all borg info commands. This prevents lock contention when multiple operations try to access SSH repositories simultaneously. Enable if you see "Repository is locked" errors.
betaFeatures.enableBypassLocksList: Enable bypass-lock for all borg list commands
betaFeatures.bypassLocksListDescription: Adds --bypass-lock to all borg list commands. This prevents lock contention when multiple read operations (like info + list) run simultaneously. Enable if you see "Failed to acquire lock" errors. Note: May show stale data if a backup is actively running.
betaFeatures.showLegacyRestoreTabLabel: Show the dedicated Restore tab in navigation
betaFeatures.showLegacyRestoreTabDescription: Enable this to access the legacy Restore tab. Restore functionality is now integrated into the Archives page, but this option allows you to use the old interface if preferred.
betaFeatures.borg2FastBrowseDescription: Uses depth-limited browsing for Borg 2 repositories to reduce payload size and improve responsiveness on very large archives. Folder sizes are hidden in this mode because recursive totals are not available.
betaFeatures.mqttIntegrationDescription: Activates MQTT integration in the UI.

exportImport.title: Export / Import

export.title: Export Configuration
export.subtitle: Download your current configuration
export.description: Export your repositories, backup schedules, and settings to a borgmatic-compatible YAML file.

import.title: Import Configuration
import.subtitle: Restore configuration from a file
import.description: Import borgmatic YAML configurations or BorgScale exports. Supports both standard borgmatic format and BorgScale exports.

exportImport.selectAtLeastOneRepo: Please select at least one repository to export
exportImport.selectFileToImport: Please select a file to import
exportImport.selectYamlFileError: Please select a YAML file (.yaml or .yml)
exportImport.includeSchedules: Include backup schedules and retention policies
exportImport.importSecurityWarning: SSH keys and repository passphrases cannot be imported for security reasons. You will need to configure them manually after import.

logManagement.title: Log Management
logManagement.failedToSaveSettings: Failed to save log management settings

mountsManagement.title: Active Mounts
mountsManagement.subtitle: Manage mounted Borg archives. Archives are automatically unmounted on container restart.

sourceDirectories.title: Source Directories & Files
sourceDirectories.subtitle: Specify which directories or files to backup to this repository
sourceDirectories.warning: At least one source directory or file is required. Add the directories or files you want to backup.

excludePatterns.title: Exclude Patterns (Optional)
excludePatterns.hint: Specify patterns to exclude from backup (e.g., *.log, *.tmp, __pycache__, node_modules)

compressionSettings.title: Compression Settings
compressionSettings.algorithmLz4: lz4 - Very high speed, very low compression (default)
compressionSettings.algorithmZlib: zlib - Medium speed, medium compression (default level 6)
compressionSettings.algorithmLzma: lzma - Low speed, high compression (default level 6)
compressionSettings.levelHelperAuto: Auto algorithm uses lz4 as fallback. Level setting not applicable.
compressionSettings.levelHelperZstd: zstd: Level 1-22. Higher = better compression but slower.
compressionSettings.levelHelperZlib: zlib: Level 0-9. Level 0 means no compression (use "none" instead).
compressionSettings.levelHelperLzma: lzma: Level 0-9. Levels above 6 are pointless and waste CPU/RAM.
compressionSettings.levelHelperDefault: Leave empty to use default level.
compressionSettings.autoDetectDesc: Uses lz4 to test if data is compressible. For incompressible data (e.g., media files), uses "none". For compressible data, uses your selected algorithm.
compressionSettings.obfuscateHelper: Level 1-6 (small overhead), 110-123 (up to 8MB additive), or 250 (Padmé, ≤12% overhead). Requires encryption.
compressionSettings.obfuscateErrorInvalid: Invalid obfuscation level. Must be 1-6, 110-123, or 250 (Padmé).

analyticsConsent.title: Help Improve BorgScale
analyticsConsent.message: We collect anonymous usage data to understand how BorgScale is used and make it better. No personal information, IP addresses, hostnames, or backup data is ever collected.
analyticsConsent.viewDashboardSuffix: to see exactly what we collect.
analyticsConsent.changeAnytime: You can change this anytime in Settings → Preferences

runningBackups.title: Running Backups
runningBackups.realtimeProgress: Real-time progress for scheduled backup jobs

scheduledChecks.title: Scheduled Checks
scheduledChecks.maxDurationHint: Time limit for check operation (3600 = 1 hour)
scheduledChecks.maxDurationHintBorg2: For Borg 2, a time limit runs a partial repository-only check (3600 = 1 hour). Use 0 to include archive checks.
scheduledChecks.notificationHint: Notification settings for check jobs can be configured in Settings → Notifications
scheduledChecks.noScheduledChecksDesc: Configure automatic repository checks to ensure data integrity
scheduledChecks.needRepository: You need to create at least one repository before scheduling checks.
scheduledChecks.noHistoryDescription: Scheduled check runs will appear here once they start executing.
scheduledChecks.checkScheduleHelperText: When should this repository check run?

multiRepositorySelector.title: Select Repositories
multiRepositorySelector.selectedCountWithOrder: Selected repositories ({{count}}) - Use arrows to change backup order

wizard.noSshConnections: No SSH connections configured. Please configure SSH connections in the SSH Keys page first.

backupFlowPreview.localToLocal: Back up local data to local repository
backupFlowPreview.localToRemote: Back up local data to remote repository
backupFlowPreview.remoteToLocal: Back up remote data to local repository via SSHFS

backupConfig.remoteSshfsNote: Remote directories are mounted via SSHFS preserving their original paths. Exclude patterns work the same as local sources (e.g., */var/cache/*).

dataSource.title: Where is the data you want to back up?
dataSource.localDescription: Back up data from this server (local or mounted filesystems)
dataSource.removeRemoteDirsFirst: Remove remote directories first to switch
dataSource.remoteToRemoteDisabledTooltip: Remote-to-remote backups are not supported. Select a local repository or use the same remote machine.
dataSource.notAvailableRemoteRepo: Not available when repository is on a remote client
dataSource.remoteDescription: Back up data from a remote machine via SSH
dataSource.removeLocalDirsFirst: Remove local directories first to switch
dataSource.remoteToRemoteBody: When the repository is stored on a remote server, the data source must be the BorgScale server. Remote-to-remote backups are not supported.
dataSource.browseRemoteNote: Browse remote directories or enter full paths manually (e.g., /home/user/documents, /var/www)
dataSource.remoteSshNote: The BorgScale server will SSH into the remote machine to browse and back up the selected directories. Ensure the SSH connection is properly configured with the necessary permissions.

location.repositoryNameHelper: A friendly name to identify this repository
location.observabilityInfo: Observability-only repositories can browse and restore existing archives but cannot create new backups.
location.readOnlyStorageDesc: Enable if the storage is read-only or locked by another process (adds --bypass-lock)
location.borgUiServerDesc: Store backups on this server's local storage
location.remoteClientDesc: Store backups on a remote machine via SSH
location.remoteDisabledInfo: This repository is configured to back up data from a remote machine. Remote-to-remote backups (backing up from one remote machine to another) are not supported. Please keep the repository on the BorgScale Server.
location.repositoryPathHelper: Path where the repository will be stored
location.borgV2Warning: Borg 2 support is still in beta. Do not use it for production data yet. It is currently intended for enthusiasts and testing, and requires the borg2 binary on any remote hosts.

restoreDestination.title: Where should files be restored?
restoreDestination.subtitle: Select the destination and location for your restored files
restoreDestination.borgUiServerDesc: Restore to this server's local storage
restoreDestination.remoteMachineDesc: Restore to a remote machine via SSH
restoreDestination.sshToSshNotSupported: SSH-to-SSH restore is not supported. Only local destinations are available for SSH repositories.
restoreDestination.restoreToOriginalDescLocal: Recreate full directory structure from root (/)
restoreDestination.restoreToOriginalDescRemote: Recreate full directory structure from root (/) on the remote machine
restoreDestination.customPathHelperLocal: Choose a writable directory where files will be restored
restoreDestination.customPathHelperRemote: Choose a writable directory on the remote machine

restoreFiles.title: Select files to restore
restoreFiles.subtitle: Browse the archive and select the files or folders you want to restore
restoreFiles.noItemsFound: No items found in this directory
restoreFiles.helpText: Click files to select them, or click folders to navigate. Use the checkbox next to folders to select entire directories.

restorePath.title: Choose restore location
restorePath.subtitle: Restore files to their original paths or select a custom destination
restorePath.restoreToOriginalDesc: Recreate full directory structure from root (/)
restorePath.customPathHelper: Choose a writable directory where files will be restored
restorePath.noSshConnection: Please select an SSH connection in the previous step.

restoreReview.readyEntireArchive: Ready to restore entire archive from {{archiveName}}
restoreReview.previewNote: Preview of where your files will be restored:
restoreReview.entireArchiveNote: The entire archive will be restored with all files and directories.
restoreReview.everythingLooksGood: Everything looks good! Click "Restore Files" to begin the restore operation.

review.name: Name
review.repositoryImportNote: Repository will be verified before import. Ensure the passphrase is correct.
review.repositoryEditNote: Changes will be saved to the repository configuration.

security.repokeyDesc: Key stored in repository, protected by passphrase
security.keyfileDesc: Key stored separately, requires passphrase + keyfile
security.noneDesc: No encryption - data stored in plaintext
security.securityWarningBody: Your backup data will be stored without encryption. Anyone with access to the repository can read your files. This is not recommended for sensitive data.
security.encryptionReadonly: Encryption settings cannot be changed after repository creation.
security.passphrasePlaceholderEdit: Leave blank to keep last saved passphrase
security.passphraseHelperEdit: Optional - leave blank to keep last saved passphrase
security.passphraseHelperCreate: Keep this safe - you cannot access backups without it!
security.borgKeyfileDesc: Borg keyfiles are typically stored without a file extension in ~/.config/borg/keys/
security.keyfileUploadNote: Keyfile will be uploaded after import
security.keyfileContentNote: Keyfile content will be saved after import
security.remoteBorgPathHelper: Path to borg executable on remote (if not in PATH)

basicInfo.repositoriesHelper: Use arrows to change backup order.
basicInfo.selectAtLeastOne: Select at least one repository to continue.

maintenance.title: Maintenance Options
maintenance.subtitle: Run prune and compact after backups to manage disk space.
maintenance.info: Prune removes old archives. Compact reclaims disk space.
maintenance.pruneAfterBackupDesc: Remove old archives based on retention policy
maintenance.compactAfterPruneDesc: Reclaim disk space by freeing segments
maintenance.compactInfo: Compact reclaims disk space after prune. May take time on large repositories.

config.scheduleHelper: Click the clock icon to use the visual builder.

scripts.scheduleLevelNote: Schedule-level: runs once per schedule. Repository-level: runs for each repository.
scripts.selectRepoFirst: Select at least one repository in Step 1 to configure scripts.

repositoryStatsGrid.originalSizeTooltip: Total logical size of all backed up data across all archives before compression and deduplication
repositoryStatsGrid.compressedSizeTooltip: Total size of all archive data after compression, before deduplication across all archives
repositoryStatsGrid.numberOfFilesTooltip: Total number of files across all archives
repositoryStatsGrid.repositorySizeTooltip: Actual storage used by the repository on disk after compression and deduplication across all archives

scheduledJobsTableSection.title: All Scheduled Jobs
scheduledJobsTableSection.noJobsDesc: Create your first scheduled backup job

advancedRepositoryOptions.title: Advanced Options
advancedRepositoryOptions.subtitle: Configure remote borg path, custom flags, and backup hooks for advanced use cases
advancedRepositoryOptions.remoteBorgPathHint: Path to borg binary on remote server (leave empty for default)
advancedRepositoryOptions.customFlagsHint: Custom command-line flags for borg create (e.g., --stats, --progress, --list)
advancedRepositoryOptions.scriptsHint: Configure inline scripts or assign reusable scripts from your Script Library.

archiveBrowser.title: Browse Archive
archiveBrowser.selectionInfo: Select specific files or folders to restore. Click folders to browse, click checkboxes to select. If no selection is made, the entire archive will be restored.
archiveBrowser.archiveTooLargeDesc: This archive contains too many files to browse safely in the UI.
archiveBrowser.selectionDescription: Select specific files or folders to restore. Click folders to browse, click checkboxes to select. If no selection is made, the entire archive will be restored.
archiveBrowser.sizeLimitPre: You can increase the limit in
archiveBrowser.sizeLimitPost: , or use command-line tools like borg mount for very large archives.

archiveContents.title: Archive Contents
archiveContents.emptyArchiveDesc: No files were backed up in this archive. This may happen if the source directory was empty or inaccessible during the backup.

commandPreview.initRepositoryDesc: Creates encrypted repository at the specified location
commandPreview.mountDisplayText: Mounts remote directory under shared temp root
commandPreview.mountDisplayTextMultiple: Mounts {{count}} unique directories under shared temp root (files share parent mount)
commandPreview.archivesPreserve: Archives preserve original paths (excludes work intuitively)
commandPreview.cleanupDesc: Unmounts remote directory after backup completes
commandPreview.backupSourceDirs: Backs up source directories to the repository
commandPreview.futureBackups: This command will be used for future backups

packages.title: System Packages
packages.subtitle: Install system packages that can be used in your backup scripts. Packages are automatically reinstalled when the container is recreated.
packages.hint: Simply enter a package name (e.g., wakeonlan, curl) and it will be installed using sudo apt-get install -y. For advanced cases, you can enable custom install commands.
packages.emptyDesc: Add system packages to use in your backup scripts

fields.packageNameHintSimple: e.g., wakeonlan, curl, jq (will run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y <name>)
fields.installCommandHint: e.g., apt-get install -y wakeonlan or pip install some-package
fields.descriptionHint: Brief description of what this package does

deleteDialog.title: Delete Package
deleteDialog.message: Are you sure you want to remove "{{name}}" from the list?
deleteDialog.note: This will not uninstall the package from the system, only remove it from the list.

resultDialog.installingDesc: Installing package... This may take a few minutes.

repositoryScriptsSection.createFirst: Create the repository first to add library scripts

scriptEditor.timeoutHint: Maximum time to wait for script execution

scriptSelector.title: Schedule-Level Scripts
scriptSelector.subtitle: These scripts run once per schedule (e.g., wake server before all backups, shutdown after)
scriptSelector.preBackup: Pre-Backup Script (runs once before all backups)
scriptSelector.postBackup: Post-Backup Script (runs once after all backups)
scriptSelector.runRepoScriptsDesc: If enabled, each repository's pre/post scripts will run during its backup

terminalLogViewer.title: Backup Logs
terminalLogViewer.showingLast: Showing last 500 of {{total}} lines (errors typically appear at end)
terminalLogViewer.newLogsAvailable: New logs available - Click to scroll to bottom

fileExplorer.sshInfoAlert: SSH connections are shown below. Configure mount points in SSH Keys page for cleaner display names.

cache.subtitle: Monitor and configure the Redis-based archive caching system for faster browsing
cache.highUsageWarning: Cache usage is at {{percent}}%. Consider increasing the max size limit or clearing old entries.
cache.inMemoryWarning: Currently using in-memory cache. Redis is unavailable or not configured. In-memory cache is limited and will be lost on restart.
cache.ttlHelperText: Time before cached entries expire. Range: 1 minute to 7 days. Current: {{current}}
cache.maxSizeHelperText: Maximum cache size. Range: 100 MB to 10 GB. Current: {{current}} GB
cache.redisUrlHelperText: Format: redis://[password@]host:port/db, rediss:// for TLS, or unix:// for Unix sockets
cache.ttlNoteText: TTL changes only affect new cache entries. Existing entries keep their original TTL until they expire.
cache.clearConfirmCount: This will remove all {{count}} cached archive entries. The next time you browse an archive, it will need to rebuild the cache (60-90 seconds for large archives).
cache.clearConfirmQuestion: Are you sure you want to continue?
cache.pleaseEnterRedisUrl: Please enter a Redis URL first

mqtt.subtitle: Configure MQTT broker connection for Home Assistant state publishing
mqtt.connectionDescription: Configure connection to your MQTT broker.
mqtt.brokerUrlHelper: MQTT broker hostname or IP address
mqtt.brokerPortHelper: MQTT broker port (1883 for standard, 8883 for TLS)
mqtt.qosLevelHelper: 0 = at most once, 1 = at least once, 2 = exactly once
mqtt.tlsConfigDescription: Configure TLS/SSL for secure MQTT connections. Required for encrypted MQTT brokers.
mqtt.dockerVolumesWarning: Certificate paths are relative to the container's filesystem. For Docker deployments, mount your certificates to a volume and reference the container path (e.g., /certs/ca.crt).

repositoryInfoDialog.noArchivesDescription: This repository has been initialized but contains no archives. Storage statistics will appear here after you create your first backup.
repositoryInfoDialog.failedToLoad: Failed to load repository information. Make sure the repository is accessible and properly initialized.

analyticsBanner.viewDashboardSuffix: to see exactly what we collect.
analyticsBanner.changeAnytimeNote: You can change this anytime in Settings → Preferences

repositoryScripts.confirmRemove: Remove this script from the repository?

tooltips.parametersOutOfSync: Parameters need attention - script definition has changed

dialog.continueOnErrorHelperText: Override default: If checked, the backup will proceed even if this script fails.

mounts.infoAlert: Note: Mounted archives appear as read-only filesystems in your file manager. Access them at the mount point path. Mounts are automatically cleaned up on container restart.
mounts.emptyDescription: Mount archives from the Archives page to browse their contents directly in your file manager.

auth.proxyHeaderRequired: Reverse proxy authentication header "{{header}}" is required
auth.localPasswordRequired: This account must use built-in password authentication to manage TOTP
auth.totpNotEnabled: Two-factor authentication is not enabled for this account
auth.passkeyUserVerificationRequired: Passkey verification is required on this device. Unlock the device with Touch ID, Face ID, or the device passcode and try again.
auth.passkeysUnavailable: Passkeys are unavailable because the WebAuthn dependency is not installed

repo.parentDirectoryNotWritable: Parent directory is not writable: {{path}}. Please ensure the directory exists with correct permissions.
repo.permissionDeniedCreateDirectory: Permission denied: cannot create directory at '{{path}}'. To store repositories on your host machine, use paths starting with '/local/'.
repo.permissionDeniedCreateParentDirectory: Permission denied: cannot create parent directory '{{path}}'. Please ensure the directory exists on your host machine with proper permissions.
repo.encryptedPassphraseRequired: Passphrase is required for encryption mode '{{mode}}'
repo.encryptedPassphraseIncorrect: Repository is encrypted but the passphrase is incorrect or missing. Please provide the correct passphrase.
repo.encryptionKeyfileNotRequired: Repository uses '{{mode}}' encryption, which does not require a keyfile. Keyfile upload is only needed for 'keyfile' or 'keyfile-blake2' encryption modes.
repo.sshConnectionNotFound: SSH connection with ID {{id}} not found
repo.atLeastOneSourceDirRequired: At least one source directory is required for full mode repositories
repo.cannotSwitchToFullModeNoSourceDirs: Cannot switch to full mode: at least one source directory is required
repo.cannotDeleteRepoWithArchives: Cannot delete repository with existing archives. Please delete all archives first.
repo.checkAlreadyRunning: A check operation is already running for this repository
repo.compactAlreadyRunning: A compact operation is already running for this repository
repo.breakLockTimeout: Timeout while trying to break lock
repo.sshUrlWithoutConnectionId: Path appears to be an SSH URL but no connection_id provided. Please select an SSH connection.
repo.pruneAlreadyRunning: A prune operation is already running for this repository

backup.cancelledByUserProcessNotFound: Backup cancelled by user (process not found, may have already completed)
backup.noLogsAvailable: No logs available for this backup (successful backups don't save logs)
backup.cannotDownloadLogsForRunningBackup: Cannot download logs for running backup

restore.cancelledByUserProcessNotFound: Restore cancelled by user (process not found, may have already completed)
restore.sshToSshNotSupported: Restoring from SSH repository to SSH destination is not supported

schedule.noRepositoriesConfigured: Scheduled job has no repositories configured
schedule.observabilityOnlyRepo: Cannot schedule backups for observability-only repositories

ssh.invalidKeyType: Invalid key type. Must be one of: {{types}}
ssh.keyGeneratedButDeployFailed: SSH key generated but deployment failed: {{error}}
ssh.failedRetrieveSystemSshKey: Failed to retrieve system SSH key: {{error}}
ssh.failedGenerateSystemSshKey: Failed to generate system SSH key: {{error}}
ssh.failedImportSystemSshKey: Failed to import system SSH key: {{error}}

settings.atLeastOneSettingRequired: At least one setting must be provided
settings.cannotDeleteLastAdmin: Cannot delete the last admin user
settings.invalidLogSavePolicy: Invalid log_save_policy. Must be one of: {{policies}}
settings.logSizeTooSmall: log_max_total_size_mb must be at least 10 MB
settings.failedGetLogStorageStats: Failed to get log storage statistics: {{error}}

activity.adminOnlyDelete: Only administrators can delete job entries
activity.cannotDeleteRunningJob: Cannot delete running job. Cancel it first or wait for completion.
activity.cannotDownloadLogsForRunningJob: Cannot download logs for running job
activity.noLogsAvailableForJob: No logs available for this job

archives.deleteAlreadyRunning: Delete operation is already running for this archive (Job ID: {{jobId}})

borg.repositoryDoesNotExist: Repository does not exist at the specified path
borg.repositoryAlreadyExists: Repository already exists at this location
borg.repositoryCheckNeeded: Repository check needed before operations can continue
borg.lockTimeout: Could not acquire repository lock. The repository is locked by another process or has a stale lock
borg.archiveAlreadyExists: Archive with this name already exists

browse.archiveMemoryTooHigh: Archive memory too high: {{estimatedMb}} MB estimated (max {{maxMb}} MB)

filesystem.sshParamsRequired: SSH key ID, host, and username are required for SSH browsing

mounts.canOnlyUnmountBorgMounts: Can only unmount Borg archive mounts via this endpoint
mounts.mountInfoNotFound: Mount succeeded but info not found
mounts.mountUnavailable: Archive mounting is unavailable in this environment

scripts.scriptExecutionTimedOut: Script execution timed out (30 second limit)
scripts.cannotEditTemplateScript: Cannot edit template scripts. Create a copy instead.
scripts.scriptInUse: Cannot delete script: it is used in {{count}} {{places}}: {{repos}}
scripts.scriptAlreadyAssigned: Script '{{name}}' is already assigned to this repository as {{hookType}}

service.backupWarningPostHooksFailed: Backup succeeded with warning but post-backup hooks failed: {{failed}}/{{total}} scripts failed
service.failedPrepareSourcePaths: Failed to prepare source paths: all SSH mounts failed or no valid paths
service.postBackupHooksFailed: Backup succeeded but post-backup hooks failed: {{failed}}/{{total}} scripts failed
service.warningRemoteProcessMayBeRunning: (Warning: Remote process may still be running, manual verification recommended)
service.postBackupHooksAlsoFailed: Post-backup hooks also failed: {{failed}}/{{total}} scripts failed
service.restoreFailedZeroFiles: Restore failed: 0 files extracted (exit code {{exitCode}}). Check logs for details.
service.restoreFailedZeroFilesPermission: Restore failed: 0 files extracted (exit code {{exitCode}}). Permission denied - you may need root access or different destination path.
service.restoreFailedZeroFilesPathNotFound: Restore failed: 0 files extracted (exit code {{exitCode}}). Path not found in archive or destination doesn't exist.
service.restoreFailedZeroFilesNoOutput: Restore failed: 0 files extracted (exit code {{exitCode}}). No error output from borg. Files may not exist in archive or permission denied.

repo.lockBroken: Lock successfully broken. You can now retry your operation.
repo.lockRemoved: Lock successfully removed. You can now start a new backup.
repo.repositoryAlreadyExists: Repository already exists at this location and has been added to the UI

ssh.sshKeyGeneratedAndDeployed: SSH key generated and deployed successfully

settings.logCleanupCompleted: Log cleanup completed. Deleted {{count}} files ({{sizeMb}} MB freed)
settings.logCleanupNoop: Log cleanup completed. No files matched the current retention or size rules.

repo.repositoryLocked: Repository is locked by another process or has a stale lock
